revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:府守神社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Fumori Shrine |image = File:Fumori-jinja shaden.JPG |location = Fuchū 1089, Wakayama City, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|34.267986|N|135.233694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}|status = Presumed {{ill|Kii Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province|qq=}} {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Village shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} |establishment = Traditionally, 875 CE (Jōgan 17) |architecture = Nagare-zukuri |alias = Seitengu |festivals = October 5 (Autumn Grand festival) |location = {{ill|Wakayama|qid=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Fumori Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|府守神社}}, ''Fumori Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Wakayama City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama City|qq=}}, Japan. It is presumed to be the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Kii Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province|qq=}}. The shrine was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. Its alternate name, "Seitengu," reflects historical Buddhist influence. == Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (倭武命) == History == According to legend, Fumori Shrine was established in 875 CE (Jōgan 17) as a guardian shrine for the {{ill|Kii Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province|qq=}} provincial capital, located to the southwest.[http://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1016 Fumori Shrine] - Wakayama Shrine Association. The site aligns with the direction of the {{ill|Kimon|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}} (northeast, considered an inauspicious direction) of the provincial capital. Archaeological excavations at the Fuchū site suggest that the area included a large administrative complex dating back to the {{ill|Nara period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}, supporting the idea that this was the location of the provincial capital.{{PDFlink|[https://w-bunkasports.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/12/725767a55babcc059e74bc941703beef.pdf Fuchū Site Excavation Report]}} - Wakayama Cultural Sports Foundation. The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Kii no Kuni Shinjamei-chō|ja|紀伊国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni Shinjamei-chō|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17210008}} as "Fumori no Kami" Ranked at Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade. The shrine's name, "Fumori," signifies its role as a guardian of the provincial capital.{{Harvid|Fumori Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}} During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine's original name was restored after a period during which it was known as Seitengu, a name linked to Buddhist influences. == festivals == * '''Bon Odori''' (August) * '''Autumn Grand festival''' (October 5) == Precincts == * '''Ushi Shrine''' (Auxiliary Shrine) File:Fuchu Site and Fumori-jinja in 2019.jpg|Fuchū Archaeological Site and Fumori Shrine (background) File:Fumori-jinja, torii-2.jpg|Torii and Monument to the Kii Provincial Capital == Related Sites == * Remains of the {{ill|Provincial capital|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial capital|qq=}} of Kii Province * {{ill|Ōyatsuhime Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyatsuhime Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine that absorbed other local shrines, including the former Sōja Shrine of Kii. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} * [[Ichinomiya]] * {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii|1=Kii Province}} {{ill|Provinces of Japan|en|Provinces of Japan|qid=Q860290|lt=Province|1=Provinces of Japan}} [[Ichinomiya]] **[[Hinokuma Shrine|Hinokuma Jingū]] **[[Niutsuhime Shrine]] **[[Itakiso shrine|Itakiso Shrine]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Fumori-jinja|qq=draft}} * [http://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1016 Fumori Shrine - Wakayama Shrine Association] {{Shinto}} {{Sōja shrines}} [[File:Fumori-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]] {{translated page|ja|府守神社|version=92301545|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:府守神社]] {{Commons category|Fumori-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11486084}} [[Category:Kii Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
